title: dryopteris oreopteris var. hesperia
intro: Dryopteris oreopteris var. hesperia, also known as Western golden-Backed Oak Fern, is a variety of fern native to the western regions of North America, characterized by its unique golden-colored fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable resource for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Dryopteris oreopteris var. hesperia include relief from arthritis, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the dried fronds in hot water to create a tea, or by mixing the dried plant material with other herbs to create a medicinal tincture.
